title: Burska

'''Burska''' was a little-known tundra [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Freestanding subsectors/Legends|freestanding subsectors]]. It had frequent, large-diameter [[Water/Legends|hail]] and was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a thick-[[Leather/Legends|hided]], passive [[Burskan gundark|offshoot]] of the [[Gundark/Legends|gundark]] [[Species/Legends|species]].

The Burskan gundark was discovered by an [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] and geological [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] team that visited the planet during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Although the first contact ended disastrously for the [[researcher]]s, one of the local gundarks was subsequently transported off-world for study, with Burska being declared a Republic-protected zone. Eventually, the planet became a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Description==
Burska was a small [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Wizards" /> that was located in the [[Burska system/Legends|Burska system]]. It lay within the [[Freestanding subsectors/Legends|freestanding subsectors]] portion of<ref name="EAOC" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], near the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. The planet was a tundra world with sparse [[Plant/Legends|vegetation]], and its terrain also included shallow [[Cave/Legends|caves]]. Neither soft nor fluffy textures existed on Burska, and decomposing plant matter was rarely encountered on the planet's tundra. [[Water/Legends|Hail]], often measuring fifteen [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] in diameter, occurred frequently on Burska. The planet had long and [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] [[Winter/Legends|winters]], which gave way to [[Spring/Legends|spring]], during which the local fauna underwent [[Reproduction/Legends|mating]] season.<ref name="Wizards" />

The dominant [[Species/Legends|species]] on Burska was the [[Burskan gundark]], an [[Subspecies/Legends|offshoot]] of the<ref name="Wizards" /> [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]], [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] [[Primate/Legends|primate]] known as the [[Gundark/Legends|gundark]], which [[Homeworld/Legends|originated]] on the<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="EAOC Vanqor">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Uziel system]]</ref> world [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]].<ref name="NEGAS" /> The Burskan variant of the gundark was an unusual, passive creature that had evolved a very thick [[Leather/Legends|hide]] in order to protect itself against the planet's fierce hail. Burskan gundarks were most active in the spring, when they wandered the tundra to forage on plant matter and [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] smaller creatures that became careless during mating season.<ref name="Wizards" />

==History==
===First contact===
For a long [[Time/Legends|time]], Burska remained a little-known world. That changed during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the existence of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], when an [[Scout ship/Legends|exploratory starship]] arrived on the planet. The [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] was carrying [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who had come to study Burska's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] and geology. To the research team's surprise, it discovered the Burskan gundarks. Subsequently, one of the [[Geologist/Legends|geologists]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] and expensive equipment was lost when the scientists' attempt to prod one of the gundarks into the ship's cargo hold resulted in the creature becoming aggressive.<ref name="Wizards" />

Better-trained [[Zoologist/Legends|zoologist]] teams later visited Burska and captured one of the local gundarks, taking the creature back to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to study it and declaring the planet to be a Republic-protected zone. Only a few of the Burskan gundarks were taken off-world for study purposes, and although various criminal enterprises attempted to use the gundarks for illicit purposes, the creatures' passivity made them unprofitable, and therefore most members of the species peacefully remained on their homeworld, retaining a level of anonymity from the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="Wizards" />

===Later history===
{{RPGseed}}
At some point, the [[Dreskin Foundation]], a zoological society based on<ref name="Wizards" /> the Core Worlds planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], hired a group of individuals to [[Mission to Burska|travel]] to Burska and bring back one of the local gundarks for study. The operation was hampered by the radical environmental protection group known as the [[Natural Planetary Protectorate]], which was dedicated to the protection of the Burskan gundark population. The NPP damaged the starship and other equipment of the Dreskin Foundation's agents, and the foundation experienced further difficulties with both their adversaries and the gundark it attempted to transport.<ref name="Wizards" />
{{Endgame}}

Scientists eventually established [[Foodstuff/Legends|feeding]] stations across Burska, which they believed contributed to the rise of the Burskan gundark population, estimated to be approximately 14,000 creatures.<ref name="Wizards" /> Burska fell within the borders of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> and at some point no earlier than [[36 ABY]], the planet was mentioned in a [[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species (book)|guide]] to various species of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] that was based on the research notes of [[sentientologist]] [[Mammon Hoole]].<ref name="NEGAS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Burska was introduced in "[[Burskan Gundark]]," an article written by [[Jeff Quick]] and published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[August 7]], [[2003]]. The article includes a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure seed to be used with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' that is set on Burska. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Wizards" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Burska [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square I-7.<ref name="TEA Burska" />
